items.front: JO 7110.65AA, dated April 20, 2023, and all changes to it.
Supplement vs. replace: how do LOAs relate to FAA Order JO 7110.65?
items.back: LOAs only supplement the order; they do not replace it. Their minima may not be less than the order's unless military authority authorizes reduced separation between military aircraft.

items.front: Three conditions that must be met before an LOA can specify minima below the order.
items.back: 1) Appropriate military authority must authorize it; 2) It must involve reduced separation; 3) It must be between military aircraft. Civilian facilities may never use an LOA to lower minima below the order.

items.front: What two things must justify a waiver to FAA Order JO 7110.65?
items.back: Exceptional or unusual requirements that dictate procedural deviations or supplementary procedures.

items.front: If military operations or facilities are involved in a waiver, what must happen first?
items.back: Prior approval by the appropriate military headquarters (per 1-1-11b). FAA interface comes after.

items.front: Where are waiver approval procedures covered?
items.back: FAA Order JO 7210.3, Facility Operation and Administration, Chapter 19, Section 7.

items.front: Physical location, not service area office location.
items.back: When referencing a non-ATO FAA regional office, which region does a facility contact under 1-1-13?

items.front: Three examples of non-ATO FAA regional office organizations listed in 1-1-13.
items.back: Communications Center, Flight Standards, and Airport offices.

items.front: Three responsibilities organizations have when the order changes.
items.back: Viewing, downloading, and subscribing to receive email notifications (per 1-1-14b).

items.front: Which five distribution targets does 1-1-14a list for this order?
items.back: Washington headquarters, regional offices, service area offices, William J. Hughes Technical Center, Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center, all air traffic field facilities, and international aviation field offices.
